userMessage: can you see m yscope what do you see?

response.text:
Yes — from the screenshot, I can see your scope display.

What I see:
- Ch 1, yellow: a roughly 24.24 MHz square-like waveform
- Ch 2, cyan: a lower-amplitude analog waveform at the same repetition rate
- Math 1, orange: Ch1 - Ch2
- Horizontal scale: 100 ns/div
- Acquisition: Single
- Scope status appears Offline in the screenshot UI

Measurements shown:
- Meas 1: Frequency on Ch 1
  - current mean: 24.243 MHz
  - long-term mean: 25.018 MHz
- Meas 2: Rise Time on Ch 1
  - current mean: 3.1993 ns
  - long-term mean: 3.5739 ns

Plots:
- Plot 1: Histogram of Meas 1, spread around about 24 to 26 MHz
- Plot 2: Spectrum of Meas 1

Vertical settings visible:
- Ch 1: 260 mV/div, 1 MΩ
- Ch 2: 250 mV/div, 1 MΩ
- Math 1: 450.7 mV/div

A quick interpretation:
- Ch 1 looks like a digital/clock signal with some edge noise/ringing
- Ch 2 looks more sinusoidal/distorted
- Math 1 highlights the difference between them and gets noisier toward the right side of the capture

If you want, I can also connect to the live instrument directly and grab a fresh screenshot from the scope to confirm what’s on it right now.
